scope: MainBoard + PD Board + BMS Board + Auxiliary Board

pins.GPIO6_alias: ESP_GPIO7 on same node at this connector

path_segments: MainBoard J1 pin01 -> BMS J1 pin01
BMS J3 pin08 -> PD J2 pin08
MainBoard J1 pin03 -> BMS J1 pin03
BMS J3 pin10 -> PD J2 pin10
MainBoard J1 pin05 -> BMS J1 pin05
BMS J1 pin05 GPIO7 -> BMS J2 pin1
resolved_cross_board_paths.firmware_meaning: External harness / toggle line, exact function not yet locked

path_segments: MainBoard J1 pin08 -> BMS J1 pin06
BMS J1 pin06 GPIO6 -> BMS J2 pin2
resolved_cross_board_paths.firmware_meaning: External harness / toggle line, exact function not yet locked

path_segments: PD board negotiates and generates VSINK
BMS board receives VBUS from PD board and VBAT from battery input
TPS2121 selects source and generates VSYS
BMS J1 pin10 VSYS -> MainBoard J1 pin10 VIN
resolved_cross_board_paths.firmware_meaning: System supply path, not a direct MCU logic interface

partially_resolved_paths.issue: The extracted net names across MainBoard and BMS/PD indicate the differential path exists, but the exact pin-by-pin naming continuity is not fully proven from the current semantic extraction.
partially_resolved_paths.firmware_impact: Low; this affects board-level USB routing understanding more than supervisory control logic.
partially_resolved_paths.required_action: Verify against source CAD or a higher-confidence page re-read before using as a locked system map.

unresolved_cross_board_interfaces.issue: The Auxiliary board host-side 5-pin connector is electrically defined, but its mating connector / landing point on the MainBoard is not identified in the current schematic set.
unresolved_cross_board_interfaces.firmware_impact: Trigger/button/LED/haptic final interface cannot yet be locked to concrete ESP32 GPIO numbers.

unresolved_cross_board_interfaces.issue: Button electrical mapping on the Auxiliary board is clear, but the system-level definition of which button is the laser trigger and how that bus reaches the ESP32 is not yet locked.
unresolved_cross_board_interfaces.firmware_impact: Trigger should remain an unresolved external interface in the system contract.

unresolved_cross_board_interfaces.issue: No explicit distance-sensor IC or dedicated distance interface is locked across the current board set.
unresolved_cross_board_interfaces.firmware_impact: Distance remains outside the locked interface set.

system_level_conclusions: GPIO4 and GPIO5 are now system-level cross-board signals to the PD controller, not generic spare GPIOs.
GPIO6 and GPIO7 are system-level cross-board signals to the BMS battery-toggle header, not generic spare GPIOs.
The Auxiliary board adds a separate host-side I2C plus interrupt interface, but that host landing point is not yet traced to the MainBoard.
The STUSB4500 is reachable through a physical cross-board chain, but USB-PD protocol ownership remains in hardware, not in the ESP32 firmware.
